title: Google Analytics
text:
This page explains how Google Analytics can be used to measure website performance and understand visitor behavior.


Google Analytics helps marketers track how people find and interact with a website. For a portfolio website, this information can show which pages attract the most attention and how visitors move through the site.






Metric
Why It Matters




Page views
Shows which pages are being visited


Traffic sources
Shows where visitors came from


Engagement rate
Shows how actively visitors interact with the site


Average engagement time
Shows how long visitors spend on pages


Most visited pages
Shows which content is most effective






For this portfolio website, I would use Google Analytics to understand whether visitors are engaging with my professional profile, project page, dashboard, and presentation.
The most important pages to track would be:

Home page
Marketing Projects page
Dashboard page
Presentation page




If the Marketing Projects page receives the most views, that may suggest visitors are most interested in my project experience.
If visitors leave quickly from the Home page, I may need to improve the introduction, layout, or call-to-action.
If the Dashboard page receives strong engagement, that may show that visitors are interested in my marketing analytics skills.



Google Analytics allows marketers to make data-driven improvements. By reviewing website traffic and engagement data, I can improve my portfolio and better understand what content is most valuable to visitors.



I have completed the Google Analytics 4 (GA4) certification, which demonstrates my understanding of website tracking, user behavior analysis, and marketing performance metrics.

title: Campus Product Launch Performance Dashboard
text:
This dashboard shows how marketing performance could be measured for my campus product launch project. The purpose is to evaluate customer interest, sales performance, and campaign effectiveness.





Metric
Sample Result




Students approached
40


Interested students
26


Products sold
18


Conversion rate
45%


Total revenue
$54









Stage
Number of Students




Students approached
40


Interested students
26


Products sold
18









Feedback Category
Percentage




Useful
40%


Affordable
30%


Convenient
20%


Other
10%






The sample dashboard suggests that the product attracted interest from many students. Out of 40 students approached, 18 purchased the product, resulting in a sample conversion rate of 45%.
The feedback also shows that students responded most positively to the product’s usefulness and affordability. These insights suggest that future marketing messages should focus on practical value and price.



This dashboard demonstrates how basic marketing data can help evaluate performance and guide future decisions. By tracking customer interest, sales, and feedback, marketers can better understand what motivates customers to purchase.

title: Marketing Projects
text:
This project involved working with a group to create a product designed to help students on campus. After developing the product idea, our team brought it to school and sold it directly to students in front of the classroom.



The goal was to apply marketing concepts in a real selling environment by identifying student needs, presenting the product, and interacting with potential customers.



Our target audience was college students who could benefit from a useful and convenient campus product.




Helped develop the product idea
Participated in planning the sales approach
Communicated with student customers
Observed customer reactions and feedback




Our strategy focused on direct selling and face-to-face communication. We explained the product’s value, answered questions, and adjusted our approach based on how students responded.



This project helped me understand how important audience insights, product positioning, and communication are in marketing. It also showed me how customer feedback can influence marketing decisions in real time.







This project focuses on analyzing social media engagement to understand what type of content performs best.




Analyze engagement metrics (likes, comments, shares)
Identify high-performing content
Suggest improvements for future posts





Excel / Google Sheets





Posts with visuals tend to perform better
Engagement increases when content is relatable to students
Timing of posts affects performance




This project strengthened my ability to interpret data and apply insights to improve marketing strategies.
